question: Does the image depict an analog circuit schematic using standard electrical symbols?
Is the circuit clearly arranged as a two-stage amplifier configuration?
Are there exactly two NPN transistors (symbolized with the arrow on the emitter pointing outward)?
Are the transistors labeled specifically as "Q1" and "Q2"?
Are resistors labeled with designations ranging from "R1" to "R8"?
Is there a specific load resistor labeled "RL" at the output stage?
Are capacitors labeled with designations ranging from "C1" to "C4"?
Is there a sine wave symbol indicating the signal source at the input?
Is there an input coupling capacitor visually connecting the signal source to the first amplifier stage?
Are the two amplifier stages connected through an interstage coupling capacitor?
Does each transistor stage include base bias resistors and a collector load resistor?
Do the emitter legs of the transistors contain a resistor and a bypass capacitor connected in parallel?
Is the power supply rail at the top labeled with "+12V"?
Are parameter values (numbers/units like kΩ, µF) displayed next to the component designators?
Are the circuit lines straight, clean, and properly connected without breaks or 'hallucinated' floating wires?
